OkaySpace Game Engine — Changelog

v1.35.0
  - Build everything with the skybox and lighting: the sky gradient and a base
    ambient level are now per-scene render settings saved with the scene, so a
    built game (the player) renders the same sky + lighting the editor shows —
    not just an editor preview. Edit them under Stats > Environment (Sky &
    Light); the menu Skybox toggle is now per-scene too.

v1.34.0
  - Fixed the updater "doesn't always update": the GitHub raw CDN was serving a
    cached VERSION.txt / .exe. Every download now sends no-cache headers and a
    unique query string so you always get the freshly published build (editor
    self-update and the launcher both).
  - Removed PlayFab. Online services are now Steam + Multiplayer.
  - Expanded the Steam SDK surface: stat increment, achievement progress
    (auto-unlocks at the max), leaderboards (submit + ranked Top-N), Steam
    Cloud (write/read/has/delete), friend count and overlay. The Services panel
    exposes all of it; the simulation backend implements it in memory.
  - Expanded multiplayer: player names in the join handshake, a server-side
    roster (Peers()/PeerName()), peer joined/left callbacks, and SendTo(id, ..)
    targeted messages (private/RPC) alongside the existing broadcast. The
    Multiplayer panel gains a name field, roster and a simple chat box.

v1.33.0
  - Scene view: resize UI as well as move it. A selected widget shows 8 grab
    handles — drag a corner/edge to resize (respecting the Canvas scale), drag
    the body to move. Works in the 2D and 3D Scene views.
  - UIDocument is now a real little UI toolkit: reusable styles and custom
    widgets. `style name color=.. size=..` defines a USS-style class a widget
    pulls in with `class=name` (its own keys win); `define name` + an indented
    block defines a custom control that `name pos=x,y` instances and shifts.

v1.32.0
  - UI now works the Unity way: widgets created from GameObject > UI are
    automatically parented under a Canvas (created if missing) and the scene
    gets one Event System. Each Canvas scales its child widgets to the window
    (Constant Pixel Size or Scale With Screen Size), so a HUD designed at one
    resolution adapts to others. Click-to-select and drag respect that scale.
  - New "UI Toolkit"-style UIDocument: author a whole menu/HUD as OkayUI markup
    (one widget per line, indent to nest) and the engine builds the widget tree
    for you. Edit the markup + Rebuild live from the Inspector. Types: panel,
    text, button, image, slider, toggle, progress; keys: pos, size, color,
    anchor, value, on, onclick. Add via GameObject > UI > UI Document.

v1.31.0
  - Scene view: click any UI widget to select it, then drag to reposition it —
    works in BOTH the 2D and 3D Scene views (UI used to only be selectable in
    2D). World picking yields to UI so a HUD click selects the HUD.
  - New Canvas component (Unity-style CanvasScaler): Constant Pixel Size or
    Scale With Screen Size with a reference resolution and width/height match.
  - New Event System component: central UI pointer dispatch — tracks the
    hovered / pressed / selected widget and exposes IsPointerOverUI(). Added
    UIRaycast() and a uniform UIRect view of every widget.
  - New 3D Platformer starter template: perspective camera, directional light,
    a physics ground and a Character Controller 3D player (New Project > 3D
    Platformer).
  - Networking: a custom message channel (RPC / events / chat). Send(channel,
    data) fans out to every peer; receive via a handler or TakeMessages().
    The server relays client messages to all other peers.

v1.30.0
  - New Follow Target 2D component: no-code chase AI — moves toward a named
    object at a set speed and stops within a distance (enemies, homing).

v1.29.0
  - Inspector: drag an asset from the Project onto a Texture or WAV field to
    set it (Mesh Renderer texture, Sprite texture, Audio Source clip).

v1.28.0
  - Hierarchy: double-click a row (or right-click > Rename) to rename it, and
    Activate/Deactivate from the right-click menu.

v1.27.0
  - Hierarchy: drag a row onto another to re-parent it; drop it on empty space
    to unparent. Drag a prefab/scene from the Project into the Hierarchy to
    instantiate / open it.
  - Fixed: 'Show in Explorer' now opens the OS file manager instead of VS Code.

v1.26.0
  - Project drag & drop: drag an asset onto a folder (in the grid or the tree)
    to move it there.
  - New Character Controller 3D: no-code WASD movement on the ground plane with
    jump; drives a Rigidbody3D if present.

v1.25.0
  - New Character Controller 2D component: no-code Top-Down or Platformer
    movement (WASD/arrows, jump). Drives a Rigidbody2D if present.
  - Removed the old node-graph Visual Script from the editor — the no-code
    Actions system replaces it (old scenes still load).

v1.24.0
  - Visual scripting: reorder conditions and instructions with the ^ / v
    buttons on each row.

v1.23.0
  - Signals (Game Creator-style): an Actions list can use the On Message
    trigger and the `send` instruction to broadcast/react to named events.
    OkayScript can broadcast too via send_message("name"), so scripts and
    visual scripts talk to each other.

v1.22.0
  - Inspector Lock: pin the inspector to one object so it stays put while you
    click around the scene/hierarchy.
  - Toolbar tooltips on Play / Stop / Pause / Step / Build.

v1.21.0
  - Project tab shows real image thumbnails for PNG/JPG/etc. assets.
  - Right-click empty space in Project for New Folder / New Script / Show in
    Explorer.

v1.20.0
  - Scripting Reference now teaches OkayScript from scratch: a "Learn the basics"
    tutorial (variables, math, if/else, loops, start()/update(dt), functions)
    with runnable examples — open it from Help > Scripting Reference.
  - Visual scripting (Actions) expanded: On Key Up trigger; goto / stop for loops
    and early-out; spawn3 (3D), set_timescale, and more condition ops (key_down,
    mouse, has_tag, is_active, dist_gt, exists).
  - OkayScript expanded: set_emissive / set_unlit / set_specular / set_tiling /
    set_mesh_texture, set_glyph / sprite_size, call("fn") for dynamic dispatch,
    and gc_get / gc_set to share variables with the Actions visual scripting.
  - Inspector: Reset Pos / Rot / Scale buttons on Transform.
  - Project: right-click > Show in Explorer.

v1.19.0
  - No-code visual scripting (Game Creator-style): a new "Actions" component
    with a Trigger (On Start / Update / Key / Collision / Click), a list of
    Conditions (key, chance, var compare, distance, tag, exists…), and a list of
    Instructions that run top-to-bottom (move, rotate, scale, wait, set/add var,
    set color/text, velocity/impulse, emit, play sound/anim, camera/light, spawn,
    destroy, load scene, log…). Authored from dropdowns in the inspector; runs in
    the editor and the player; saved with the scene.
  - Expanded OkayScript: set_emissive / set_unlit / set_specular / set_tiling /
    set_mesh_texture, and gc_get / gc_set to share variables with the Actions
    visual scripting.

v1.18.0
  - 3D positional audio: an Audio Source can be marked 3D (spatial) with Min/Max
    Distance, and the mixer attenuates it by distance to the listener (the main
    camera). Great for world sounds that fade as you move away.

v1.17.0
  - Unity-style Project tab: a folder tree on the left, an icon grid on the
    right with type-colored thumbnails, a clickable breadcrumb, and search.
    Double-click to enter folders / open scenes / instantiate prefabs / open
    scripts. New Folder + New Script buttons, plus right-click Rename / Delete.
  - Fly the Scene camera: hold the right mouse button and use WASD to move
    (Q/E down/up, Shift to move faster) while you look around.
  - Fixed models glitching/vanishing when you zoom in very close — the renderer
    now clips triangles against the camera near plane instead of dropping them.
  - More UI polish: refined dark theme (spacing, scrollbars, contrast, rounding).

v1.16.0
  - Proper UV wrapping on Sphere and Cylinder primitives, so textures wrap
    around them cleanly instead of using the planar decal fallback.
  - Texture tiling: a per-object Tiling (U, V) control repeats the texture
    across the surface (Mesh Renderer inspector; saved with the scene).

v1.15.0
  - Textured 3D meshes: set a Texture image on a Mesh Renderer and it's mapped
    onto the mesh (planar/box projection — exact on cube/plane/quad faces),
    tinted by Color and lit by the material. Works in the editor and the
    standalone player; Build Game bundles the image. Textures are cached.

v1.14.0
  - Materials on 3D objects: the Mesh Renderer now has Emissive (self-glow),
    Specular + Shininess (Blinn-Phong highlights), and an Unlit toggle, all
    honored by the solid renderer in both the editor and the player. Tune them
    in the inspector; they save with the scene.

v1.13.0
  - File > Open Recent: quick access to recently opened/saved scenes (persisted
    between sessions).
  - File > Autosave: saves the open scene every 60s while editing.
  - Unsaved-changes guard: closing the window or choosing Exit with unsaved work
    now prompts to Save / Don't Save / Cancel instead of losing changes.

v1.12.0
  - Pause / Resume while playing (Step now pauses then advances one frame).
  - Play-mode tint: a colored border frames the window while playing (green) or
    paused (amber), so the run state is obvious at a glance.
  - Editor themes: Dark / Light / Classic under View > Theme.
  - View > Clear Console on Play (on by default) wipes the log each run.
  - Status bar shows EDIT / PLAYING / PAUSED.

v1.11.0
  - GameObject > 3D Object submenu to create any built-in primitive (Cube,
    Sphere, Cylinder, Capsule, Cone, Plane, Pyramid, Wedge, Torus, Icosphere,
    Quad).
  - "Fit to mesh" button on 3D Box/Sphere/Capsule colliders sizes them to the
    object's mesh in one click.
  - One-click "Add Physics (Rigidbody + Box)" on a Mesh Renderer adds a
    Rigidbody3D and a box collider fitted to the mesh, so a shape drops and
    collides immediately on Play.

v1.10.0
  - Edit the scene while playing: select and Move/Rotate/Scale objects in the
    Scene view during Play (changes revert on Stop, like Unity).
  - Copy / Paste GameObjects with Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V (also in the GameObject menu).
  - Default skybox: a sky gradient now fills the 3D view background (toggle:
    View > Skybox).
  - IDE: every panel tab now has a close button, and View > Reset Layout
    restores the default docked workspace and reopens all panels.
  - Scripting API expanded a lot: 3D rigidbody control (set_velocity3,
    add_force3, jump…), facing helpers (forward_x/y/z, move_forward/right),
    object identity (name/tag/set_active/destroy_obj), 3D raycast, fps(), and
    more — see Help > Scripting Reference and docs/SCRIPTING.md (both expanded).

v1.9.1
  - Fixed Scene/Game flicker when both 3D views are visible at once (split or
    docked, in or out of Play): the two views shared one render texture and
    fought over it each frame. Each view now renders to its own texture.

v1.9.0
  - Collider gizmos: colliders now draw as green wireframe outlines in the
    Scene view (2D box/circle/capsule and 3D box/sphere/capsule), so you can
    see and size them like Unity. Toggle under View > Colliders (gizmos).

v1.8.0
  - 3D physics: Rigidbody3D (gravity, mass, drag, bounciness, freeze axes) plus
    Box / Sphere / Capsule 3D colliders, with collision response, triggers and
    Raycast / OverlapSphere queries. Stepped automatically each frame.
  - More 2D colliders: added a Capsule Collider 2D (vertical or horizontal).
  - Scene gizmos: cameras now draw a wireframe frustum and lights draw a sun +
    direction arrow, so you can see and place them in the Scene view.
  - Scripting documentation: Help > Scripting Reference (and a Docs button in
    the Script Editor) opens a full OkayScript reference; also in docs/.
  - Scripting is OkayScript-only now (Lua removed); the language picker reflects
    that and the starter script is valid OkayScript so Compile & Run works.

v1.7.0
  - Script editor works now: the starter script is written in the correct
    syntax for the selected language (OkayScript uses C-style braces and
    semicolons, which the previous end-style template broke on).
  - The language picker is real: it lists only the backends this build
    supports, and Lua is now bundled (no external install) so selecting "lua"
    actually runs Lua. Switching language swaps in matching starter syntax.
    (C# needs Mono and isn't available in the standalone Windows build.)

v1.6.0
  - The launcher now checks the engine version on startup and auto-updates in
    the background: if a newer build is published (or a runtime is missing) it
    downloads the engine + player and stages a new launcher, with live status
    in the sidebar and a manual "Check for updates" button. Self-heals the
    "player runtime not found" case by fetching it automatically.
  - Pressing Play in the editor now jumps straight to the Game tab so you see
    your game immediately instead of staying on the Scene view.

v1.5.0
  - Play mode now feeds real keyboard / mouse / gamepad into the engine, so the
    playable templates (Platformer, Top-Down, Coin Collector, Snake) and your
    own movement scripts respond inside the editor — you can actually move now.
  - UI objects (buttons, panels, sliders, toggles, progress bars, images, text)
    now render in the 3D Scene view and the Game view, not just 2D — created UI
    is visible immediately in any project.
  - Build Game is self-contained: if the player runtime isn't sitting next to
    the editor, it's downloaded from GitHub automatically so the exported game
    runs without any manual file copying.
  - The self-updater is back, smarter: Engine > Check for Updates compares your
    version against the published one, shows the release notes, and only offers
    "Download & Install" when a newer build actually exists (no more nagging).
  - Cleaner Inspector header: auto-sized tinted bar with the active toggle, a
    colored type chip, the editable name, Tag field and Save-as-Prefab.

v1.4.0
  - Lighting (Light component + scene shading) and z-buffered solid 3D rendering.
  - Camera scaling locked (move/rotate only) to stop the select-camera flicker.
  - FiveM-style launcher (Create / Play / Marketplace).
